parts.passageTitle: Your next car may be electric

passage.text:
Your next car may be electric
Source: Tim Thwaites, Cosmos magazine.
The main reasons why electric cars are not more popular at present are their price and their relatively small range. Existing battery systems only allow electric cars to travel a distance of between 100 and 160 km. However, this distance may not be a problem for urban drivers. A recent Sydney study reported that 70 percent of journeys were 30 km or less, and recent data from the US suggests that 77 percent of trips taken there are 48 km or less.
An innovative company called Better Place is aiming to make electric cars an option for all drivers. It wants to see existing vehicles replaced by electric vehicles which, it says, offer a number of benefits. Firstly, they can be powered by renewable energy which produces zero emissions. What is more, electric motors are more efficient and can convert more than 90 percent of power into movement, whereas the efficiency of diesel or petrol engines is less than 20 percent.
The plan is simple but revolutionary. It starts with the installation of a home charge point, and through this, the vehicle will be plugged into the electricity grid whenever it is in the garage, typically at night. In the morning, with a fully charged battery, the car is capable of as much as 160 km in urban motoring conditions. In addition to the home charge point, the battery can be topped up by charge points at work and at supermarkets.
The battery is linked to a control centre by smart technology inside the vehicle. Better Place can then ensure that the car is charged with electricity from renewable sources at the cheapest price. For longer trips, a navigation system directs the driver to the nearest switch station, where the depleted battery can be replaced with a charged one by a robot within a couple of minutes.
(No comprehension questions set for this passage — reading only.)

questionGroups.instruction:
Complete the notes below.
Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER from the passage for each answer.

questions.prompt: Car connects to 1 ______ through home charge point.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

connects to
home charge point

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

"the vehicle will be plugged into the electricity grid whenever it is in the garage"

Bước 3: Phân tích

plugged into = connects to
electricity grid = nơi chiếc xe được kết nối thông qua home charge point

Đáp án: electricity grid

questions.prompt: Battery allows travel of up to 2 ______ in cities.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

travel of up to
cities

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

"the car is capable of as much as 160 km in urban motoring conditions"

Bước 3: Phân tích

as much as = up to
urban = cities

Đáp án: 160 km

questions.prompt: Battery can be recharged at 3 ................ or shops.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

recharged
shops

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

"the battery can be topped up by charge points at work and at supermarkets"

Bước 3: Phân tích

topped up = recharged
supermarkets = shops
work là địa điểm còn thiếu

Đáp án: work

questions.prompt: Car communicates with control centre to receive power lowest 4 ................
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

control centre
lowest

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

"the car is charged with electricity from renewable sources at the cheapest price"

Bước 3: Phân tích

cheapest = lowest
charged with electricity = receive power

Đáp án: price

Bước 1: Keyword

inside car
switch stations

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

"a navigation system directs the driver to the nearest switch station"

Bước 3: Phân tích

navigation system = hệ thống bên trong xe
directs the driver to = locates
Đáp án: navigation system

questions.prompt: 6 ................ at switch centre will change batteries.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

switch centre
change batteries

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

"the depleted battery can be replaced with a charged one by a robot"

Bước 3: Phân tích

replaced = changed
by a robot = robot thực hiện việc thay pin

Đáp án: robot

questionGroups.note:
• Car connects to [1] through home charge point.
 • Battery allows travel of up to [2] in cities.
 • Battery can be recharged at [3] or shops.
 • Car communicates with control centre to receive power lowest [4] 
 • [5] inside car locates switch stations.
 • [6] at switch centre will change batteries.

passage.text:
How traffic-free shopping streets developed
A The concept of traffic-free shopping areas goes back a long time. During the Middle Ages, traffic-free shopping areas known as souks were built in Middle Eastern countries to allow people to shop in comfort and, more importantly, safety. As far back as 2,000 years ago, road traffic was banned from central Rome during the day to allow for the free movement of pedestrians, and was only allowed in at night when shops and markets had closed for the day. In most other cities, however, pedestrians were forced to share the streets with horses, coaches and, later, with cars and other motorised vehicles.
B The modern, traffic-free shopping street was born in Europe in the 1960s, when both city populations and car ownership increased rapidly. Dirty exhaust fumes from cars and the risks involved in crossing the road were beginning to make shopping an unpleasant and dangerous experience. Many believed the time was right for experimenting with car-free streets, and shopping areas seemed the best place to start.
C At first, there was resistance from shopkeepers. They believed that such a move would be bad for business. They argued that people would avoid streets if they were unable to get to them in their cars. When the first streets in Europe were closed to traffic, there were even noisy demonstrations, as many shopkeepers predicted they would lose customers.
D However, research carried out afterwards in several European cities revealed some unexpected statistics. In Munich, Cologne and Hamburg, visitors to shopping areas increased by 50 percent. On Copenhagen's main shopping street, shopkeepers reported sales increases of 25–40 percent. Shopkeepers in Minneapolis, USA, were so impressed when they learnt this that they even offered to pay for the construction and maintenance costs of their own traffic-free streets.
E With the arrival of the traffic-free shopping street, many shops, especially those selling things like clothes, food and smaller luxury items, prospered. Unfortunately, it wasn't good news for everyone, as shops selling furniture and larger electrical appliances actually saw their sales drop. Many of these were forced to move elsewhere, away from the city centre. Today they are a common feature on the outskirts of towns and cities, often situated in out-of-town retail zones with their own car parks and other local facilities.

questionGroups.instruction:
Reading Passage has paragraphs A–E. Choose the correct heading for each paragraph from the list of headings below.
Write the correct number, i–vii, in boxes 1–5 on your answer sheet.

questions.explanation: EN: Paragraph A mentions '2,000 years ago, road traffic was banned from central Rome', which is an idea from ancient history. VI: Đoạn A đề cập đến việc '2.000 năm trước, giao thông đường bộ đã bị cấm ở trung tâm Rome', đây là một ý tưởng từ lịch sử cổ đại.

EN: Paragraph B talks about exhaust fumes and road safety making shopping dangerous, creating a need for change. VI: Đoạn B nói về khói thải và an toàn đường bộ khiến việc mua sắm trở nên nguy hiểm, tạo ra nhu cầu thay đổi.

EN: Paragraph C mentions 'resistance from shopkeepers' and 'noisy demonstrations' (local opposition). VI: Đoạn C đề cập đến 'sự phản đối từ các chủ cửa hàng' và 'các cuộc biểu tình ồn ào' (sự phản đối của địa phương).

EN: Paragraph D discusses stats from Europe (Munich, Copenhagen) which impressed shopkeepers in Minneapolis, USA (North America learning). VI: Đoạn D thảo luận về số liệu từ châu Âu khiến các chủ cửa hàng ở Minneapolis, Mỹ ấn tượng (Bắc Mỹ học hỏi từ châu Âu).

EN: Paragraph E details how some shops prospered (winners) while furniture/appliance stores saw sales drop and moved (losers). VI: Đoạn E chi tiết hóa việc một số cửa hàng phát đạt (người chiến thắng) trong khi các cửa hàng đồ nội thất/thiết bị điện giảm doanh số (người thua cuộc).

passage.text:
The Boeing 787 Dreamliner
Until now, airliner fuselages have been made of aluminium sheets. Large aircraft can have 1,500 of these sheets with between 40,000 and 50,000 metal fasteners. The 787 is the first airliner to be built with a one-piece fuselage made from a special material called composite. Not only does this make the airliner quicker and easier to build, but it also makes it a lot lighter. The advantage of this weight reduction is that the 787 uses 20 percent less fuel than other airliners of a similar size, which makes it much more environmentally friendly.
Sometimes an airliner needs to change from one type of engine to another. This is a difficult and time-consuming process. The 787 has a revolutionary engine attachment on the wing which means that engines can be changed in a much shorter time. The case containing the engine is also designed to cut down the noise from the engine, making it less noisy for passengers in the cabin as well as for people on the ground.
In addition to a quieter cabin, passengers will also benefit from windows which are 65 percent larger than those on other airliners, giving them a much better view. The windows also have a unique facility which allows passengers or cabin crew to control the amount of light that enters them. This is thanks to a liquid in the window which reacts to an electric current. When a passenger or cabin crew member presses a button, the current causes a chemical reaction in the window which darkens the liquid.
(No comprehension questions set for this passage — reading only.)

questionGroups.instruction:
Complete the notes below.
Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER from the passage for each answer.

questions.prompt: Composite fuselage material is much __________ than traditional aluminium sheets.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
composite fuselage material
aluminium sheets
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

"it also makes it a lot lighter"

Bước 3: Phân tích
it = composite material
lighter = nhẹ hơn aluminium sheets

Đáp án: lighter

questions.prompt: Composite fuselage material reduces the amount of __________ that the airliner needs.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
reduces the amount of
airliner needs
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

"the 787 uses 20 percent less fuel than other airliners"

Bước 3: Phân tích
uses less fuel = reduces the amount of fuel needed

Đáp án: fuel

Bước 1: Keyword
accommodate
at least
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

"carrying 210 passengers or more"

Bước 3: Phân tích
accommodate = carry
or more = at least

Đáp án: 210 passengers

questions.prompt: Engineers can fit a new engine in a __________ than it takes on other airliners.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
fit a new engine
other airliners
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

"engines can be changed in a much shorter time"

Bước 3: Phân tích
fit a new engine = change an engine
much shorter time = thời gian ngắn hơn

Đáp án: shorter time

Bước 1: Keyword
engine case
reduces
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

"It has been designed to cut down the noise from the engine"

Bước 3: Phân tích
cut down = reduce
noise from the engine

Đáp án: noise

questions.prompt: The size of the windows has increased by __________.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
size of the windows
increased by
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

"windows which are 65 percent larger"

Bước 3: Phân tích
larger = increased in size
by 65 percent

Đáp án: 65 percent

questions.prompt: Window glass can be made darker by passing an __________ through them.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
made darker
passing through them
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

"a liquid in the window which reacts to an electric current"

và

"the current causes a chemical reaction in the window which darkens the liquid"

Bước 3: Phân tích
darkens = makes darker
electric current passes through the window and causes the change

Đáp án: electric current

questionGroups.note:
- Composite fuselage material is much [1] than traditional aluminium sheets.
- Composite fuselage material reduces the amount of [2] that the airliner needs.
- Airliner can accommodate at least [3]
- Engineers can fit a new engine in a [4] than it takes on other airliners.
- A special engine case reduces [5]
- The size of the windows has increased by [6]
- Window glass can be made darker by passing an [7] through them.

parts.passageTitle: Traffic jams — no end in sight

passage.text:
Traffic jams — no end in sight
A Traffic congestion affects people throughout the world. Traffic jams cause smog in dozens of cities across both the developed and developing world. In the US, commuters spend an average of a full working week each year sitting in traffic jams, according to the Texas Transportation Institute. While alternative ways of getting around are available, most people still choose their cars because they are looking for convenience, comfort and privacy.
B The most promising technique for reducing city traffic is called congestion pricing, whereby cities charge a toll to enter certain parts of town at certain times of day. In theory, if the toll is high enough, some drivers will cancel their trips or go by bus or train. And in practice it seems to work: Singapore, London and Stockholm have reduced traffic and pollution in city centres thanks to congestion pricing.
C Another way to reduce rush-hour traffic is for employers to implement flextime, which lets employees travel to and from work at off-peak traffic times to avoid the rush hour. Those who have to travel during busy times can do their part by sharing cars. Employers can also allow more staff to telecommute (work from home) so as to keep more cars off the road altogether.
D Some urban planners still believe that the best way to ease traffic congestion is to build more roads, especially roads that can take drivers around or over crowded city streets. But such techniques do not really keep cars off the road; they only accommodate more of them.
E Other, more forward-thinking, planners know that more and more drivers and cars are taking to the roads every day, and they are unwilling to encourage more private automobiles when public transport is so much better both for people and the environment. For this reason, the American government has decided to spend some $7 billion on helping to increase capacity on public-transport systems and upgrade them with more efficient technologies. But environmentalists complain that such funding is tiny compared to the $50 billion being spent on roads and bridges.

questionGroups.instruction:
Reading Passage has paragraphs A–E. Choose the correct heading for each paragraph from the list of headings below.
Write the correct number, i–vii, in boxes 1–5 on your answer sheet.

questions.explanation: EN: Paragraph A states 'Traffic congestion affects people throughout the world' (global problem). VI: Đoạn A nêu rõ 'Tắc nghẽn giao thông ảnh hưởng đến con người trên toàn thế giới' (vấn đề toàn cầu).

EN: Paragraph B describes congestion pricing, where cities 'charge a toll to enter certain parts of town'. VI: Đoạn B mô tả việc định giá tắc nghẽn, nơi các thành phố 'thu phí để vào một số khu vực nhất định'.
EN: Paragraph C discusses flextime, car sharing, and telecommuting (changing how people work). VI: Đoạn C thảo luận về giờ làm việc linh hoạt, chia sẻ xe và làm việc từ xa (thay đổi cách làm việc).

EN: Paragraph D explains that building more roads only accommodates more cars but doesn't keep them off the road (a solution that is no solution). VI: Đoạn D giải thích rằng xây thêm đường chỉ chứa được nhiều xe hơn chứ không giúp giảm xe trên đường (một giải pháp không phải là giải pháp).

EN: Paragraph E notes that environmentalists complain that public transport funding is 'tiny' compared to road spending (not doing enough). VI: Đoạn E lưu ý rằng các nhà môi trường phàn nàn rằng tài trợ cho giao thông công cộng là 'quá nhỏ bé' so với chi tiêu cho đường bộ (chưa làm đủ).
